---
title: "2025_AFDW_emergence"
author: "Chloé Gilligan"
date: "2026-03-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(car) 
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse) 
library(dplyr)
library(lmerTest)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(dunn.test)
```

#Load data
```{r}
afdw <- read_csv("../data/2025_AFDW_sorted_filtered.csv")
meas <- read_csv("../data/NL_2025_master_measurements.csv")
```

#Merge
```{r}
afdw <- afdw %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_Code = str_extract(Full.Sample.ID, "^[^_]+"),
    Snail_Num   = as.integer(str_extract(Full.Sample.ID, "(?<=_)\\d+$"))
  )

meas_clean <- meas %>%
  select(Sample_Code = sample_name, Snail_Num = snail_number, length, width) %>%
  mutate(Snail_Num = as.integer(Snail_Num))

merged <- left_join(afdw, meas_clean, by = c("Sample_Code", "Snail_Num"))
```

#Decode color labels
```{r}
merged <- merged %>%
mutate(Treatment = recode(Color,
    "B" = "Ambient + Crab",
    "G" = "Ambient + No Crab",
    "R" = "Heated + Crab",
    "P" = "Heated + No Crab"
  ))
```

#RIG4- outlier in the 4's when everything else is a 1
```{r}
merged <- merged %>% filter(Full.Sample.ID != "RIG4_1")
```


```{r}
names(merged)
```


#Reorganizing columns
```{r}
merged <- merged %>%
  select(Tin, Full.Sample.ID, Sample_Code, Snail_Num, Treatment = Color_Label, Color,
         Pre.burned.pan, Dry.Snail.Weight, Ashed.Snails.Ashed.Tin,
         Date.weighed, Date.ashed, Tissue.Mass, Shell.Mass, length, width)

```

#Exporting merged data
```{r}
write_csv(merged, "../data/2025_AFDW_merged.csv")
```


#Tissue mass stats
```{r}
summary(aov(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
TukeyHSD(aov(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))

```

#shell mass
```{r}
summary(aov(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
TukeyHSD(aov(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
```

```{r}
#Plotting
pal <- c("Ambient + Crab" = "#1C86EE", "Ambient + No Crab" = "#B3EE3A",
         "Heated + Crab"  = "#EE0000", "Heated + No Crab"  = "#FFB6C1")

p_tissue <- ggplot(merged, aes(x = Treatment, y = Tissue.Mass, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = "Tissue Mass by Treatment", x = NULL, y = "Tissue Mass (g)") +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

p_shell <- ggplot(merged, aes(x = Treatment, y = Shell.Mass, fill = Treatment)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal) +
  labs(title = "Shell Mass by Treatment", x = NULL, y = "Shell Mass (g)") +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none", axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1))

ggarrange(p_tissue, p_shell, ncol = 2, labels = c("A", "B"))
```
```

#stats
```{r}
stats <- merged %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    mean_tissue = mean(Tissue.Mass, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tissue   = sd(Tissue.Mass, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_shell  = mean(Shell.Mass, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_shell    = sd(Shell.Mass, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(stats)
```

#stats
```{r}
# Tissue Mass
tissue_aov <- lm(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
Anova(tissue_aov, type = 3)

shell_aov <- lm(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
Anova(shell_aov, type = 3)

TukeyHSD(aov(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
TukeyHSD(aov(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged))
```

```{r}
# Check normality
shapiro.test(merged$Tissue.Mass)
shapiro.test(merged$Shell.Mass)

leveneTest(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
leveneTest(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
```
```{r}
# Kruskal-Wallis
kruskal.test(Tissue.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)
kruskal.test(Shell.Mass ~ Treatment, data = merged)

dunn.test(merged$Tissue.Mass, merged$Treatment, method = "bonferroni")
dunn.test(merged$Shell.Mass, merged$Treatment, method = "bonferroni")
```
